item.title: Account Director / Senior Account Director (Technology)
item.description: The Organisation Our client is a fast-growing, independent communications agency specialising in technology, investment, and purpose-led organisations. Now in its fourth year, the agency has built a strong reputation for working with “meaningful” organisations across climate, femtech, DEI, financial inclusion, health, and education, alongside some of the most ambitious companies shaping the future of technology. The business has grown revenue by 30% over the last financial year. With a growing international footprint, including expansion into New York, the agency supports a diverse client base spanning start ups,scale ups, investors, and institutions across multiple global markets. Its approach is grounded in thoughtful storytelling, high-quality media engagement, and a genuine respect for journalism. The Opportunity This is a key hire within a high-performing and values-driven agency at a pivotal stage of growth. You will take ownership of key client relationships from the outset, leading delivery across accounts while managing and developing a team. Alongside client work, you will contribute to the broader direction of the business, including input into strategy, operations, and new business activity. The role offers significant scope for impact, responsibility, and progression within a collaborative and intellectually curious environment. Main Responsibilities Lead the strategic direction and delivery of multiple client accounts Build trusted, senior-level relationships with clients across sectors Develop and deliver media strategies that generate high-impact coverage Drive proactive media engagement and maintain strong journalist relationships Manage, mentor, and develop junior team members Contribute to business growth through new business and organic account development Support wider agency initiatives across operations and strategy Skills & Experience Demonstrable experience managing client relationships within a PR agency environment Strong background in technology or technology-adjacent communications Proven ability to secure high-quality, top-tier media coverage Experience leading and developing teams Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of UK, US, and European media landscapes Interest in purpose, emerging technologies, including AI, and their broader impact Commercial awareness with the ability to identify growth opportunities Wider Benefits One-month paid sabbatical after four years’ service Flexible wellbeing support, including fitness or therapy subsidy Generous annual leave, including additional time over the Christmas period and a birthday day off Regular team activities and company-wide events Monthly development check-ins and access to external training Home office and technology provision Access to learning and wellbeing platforms Health-related benefits including eye care support Close To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles at leadingagencies, contact Tasmin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporateaffairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutiquesearch firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reportsto them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduringrelationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversityacross our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerationsshould they be required. ‍

item.title: Public Affairs & Policy Manager (Wales) - Not-For-Profit (Health Sector)
item.description: About the Role ‍ Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading not-for-profit organisation in the health sector to appoint a Public Affairs & Policy Manager. This is a key role leading policy and external affairs activity in Wales, with collaboration across UK-wide teams. ‍ The successful candidate will lead public affairs strategy, manage stakeholder relationships, and provide expert political and policy advice to senior leadership. You will oversee a small team and play an important role in shaping the organisation’s external engagement and policy positioning. ‍ This role offers the opportunity to influence health policy, build strong stakeholder relationships, and contribute to meaningful impact across the healthcare system. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ You will lead public affairs and policy activity in Wales, providing strategic insight and advice to internal and external stakeholders. You will build and maintain strong relationships with policymakers, government bodies, and partners across the health sector, ensuring the organisation is positioned as a credible and influential voice. ‍ You will provide high-quality political intelligence, policy analysis, briefings, and advice to senior leaders. The role will involve overseeing policy projects, consultations, stakeholder engagement, and external communications activity, while managing and supporting a small team to deliver high-quality work. Working closely with colleagues across the UK, you will contribute to a joined-up external affairs strategy and help ensure the organisation responds effectively to developments in the political and policy environment. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for someone with a strong understanding of the Welsh political, public affairs, and health policy environment. You will have experience providing policy and strategic advice to senior stakeholders and be confident in building trusted relationships with senior external partners. ‍ You will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce high-quality briefings and policy documents. You will have experience managing complex workstreams and delivering projects to a high standard, as well as the ability to lead or support a team. Welsh language skills are desirable but not essential. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via Ellwood Atfield. Suitable applicants will be invited to an initial briefing call with our team to discuss the role in more detail. ‍ Closing date: Monday 27 April 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged , as shortlisting and interviews will take place on a rolling basis. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Crisis)
item.description: ‍ The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established strategic communications consultancy. They work across a range of sectors, supporting clients through complex and often high-profile situations. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a senior role within a growing and ambitious practice, offering the opportunity to shape and develop crisis and issues communications capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in leading client work, contributing to the broader growth of the practice, and defining areas of specialism within a generalist offer. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the delivery of crisis and issues communications programmes for a range of clients Provide senior strategic counsel on reputational risk, litigation, and high-profile issues Support the development and positioning of the crisis practice within the wider business Build strong, trusted relationships with clients and key external stakeholders Oversee and develop account teams, ensuring high-quality delivery Contribute to new business activity, including pitches and proposals Maintain high standards across client work, ensuring consistency and efficiency Mentor and support the development of junior colleagues ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Strong experience in crisis, issues, and/or litigation communications (essential) Excellent understanding of the media landscape and handling of sensitive situations Proven ability to advise senior stakeholders in complex environments Demonstrated leadership capability with a collaborative approach Commercial awareness, with experience supporting business development activity Ability to operate with sound judgement in fast-paced, high-pressure situations ‍ Benefits ‍ Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Flexible and hybrid working arrangements Generous leave and wellbeing support Structured learning and development opportunities Inclusive, collaborative culture with regular social and team activities ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Associate Director (Financial Services)
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established, mid-sized communications agency with a global offering and a strong heritage in financial services. Known for its agile and collaborative culture, the agency has built a reputation for delivering high-quality, insight-led work, alongside industry recognition for its people-first approach. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a pivotal role within a high-performing financial services team, acting as the number two to the Head of Financial Services. You will play a central role in shaping client strategy, leading delivery across key accounts, and supporting the continued growth of the practice within a nimble and ambitious agency environment. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the strategic direction and delivery of integrated communications programmes for financial services clients Act as a senior counsel to clients, building trusted and long-term relationships Support and deputise for the Head of Financial Services as required Drive new business activity, including contributing to pitches and identifying growth opportunities Oversee and mentor junior team members, fostering a high-performing and supportive team culture Ensure consistently high standards of delivery across multiple accounts Collaborate across the wider agency to deliver integrated, multi-market campaigns Skills & Experience ‍ Significant experience within a communications agency, with a focus on financial services Strong understanding of the financial services landscape, including regulatory and reputational considerations Proven ability to lead client relationships and provide senior strategic counsel Experience in managing and developing high-performing teams Track record of contributing to business development and agency growth Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment Close ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Digital Senior Account Manager
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a fast-growing strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of communications, policy and reputation. With an international footprint, the firm supports senior leaders across government, business and high-profile organisations navigating complex, high-stakes environments. The team is known for its discreet, insight-led approach, combining geopolitical awareness with clear strategic execution. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for a digitally focused communications professional to join a high-performing and entrepreneurial team. You will play a key role in delivering integrated campaigns and digital strategies for clients operating in complex and often high-profile contexts. The role offers significant international exposure, opportunity for regular travel and the chance to work on high-impact mandates with senior stakeholders. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Plan and execute digital communications strategies across sensitive, high-profile client accounts Manage day-to-day social media activity, including drafting, scheduling and publishing content for senior individuals and organisations Develop digital narratives that support reputation management, public positioning and campaign objectives Monitor online conversations, media coverage and stakeholder sentiment, providing real-time insight and recommendations Support crisis and issues management through rapid-response digital content and messaging Conduct digital and stakeholder research, including audience mapping and platform analysis, to inform strategy Analyse campaign performance and produce insight-led reports to refine and optimise activity Work closely with colleagues across strategy, research and public affairs to deliver integrated client programmes Provide hands-on support to senior clients, ensuring digital output is accurate, timely and aligned with wider communications objectives Monitor political, regulatory and media landscapes to inform campaign activity ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Experience in digital communications within an agency, in-house or public sector environment Strong understanding of social media, digital campaigning and online engagement strategies Ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments with sound judgement Experience supporting senior stakeholders or working on campaigns involving complex or sensitive issues Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights Interest in geopolitics and international affairs Excellent written and verbal communication skills Likely to have 3–5 years’ relevant experience – this will not be suitable for someone more experienced and there is no flexibility here ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. item.title: Director of Communications
item.description: Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with a leading public sector regulatory body that plays a vital role in upholding information rights and data protection standards across the UK. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a rare opportunity for a senior communications leader to join an organisation at a pivotal moment and shape the way it communicates its purpose, decisions and regulatory authority to a wide range of stakeholders. ‍ Reporting to the Executive Director of Communications and Public Affairs and as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director of Communications will set the vision and strategic direction for a modern, high-performing communications function that is embedded early in organisational decision-making and will be leading a multidisciplinary team supported by two direct reports and oversee a team of30. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Set and lead the communications strategy, aligned to organisational priorities and regulatory objectives, working in close partnership with the Executive Director. Position communications as a core strategic function, embedded early in planning, policy and regulatory decision making.. Provide senior counsel to the Executive Team on sensitive, high-profile and reputationally significant issues, leading the organisation’s approach to reputation management and crisis readiness. Champion an insight-driven, outcomes-focused approach to communications, using research, data and evaluation to inform strategy and measure impact. Lead a digital first culture, ensuring channels, content and formats reflect how audiences consume information, while encouraging creativity and innovation. Own and shape the organisation’s overarching narrative and lead proactive engagement to build public trust and confidence. Work with internal communications to align internal and external messaging, supporting leadership communication capability and consistency across the organisation. Lead, inspire and develop a collaborative, high-performing team of around 30 people, fostering a culture of confidence, clarity, accountability and continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Experience Required ‍ Senior leadership experience running a substantial communications function in a high profile, scrutinised environment. Proven experience advising senior leaders on sensitive, complex and reputationally significant communications issues. A demonstrable record of using communications to shape outcomes Experience setting and delivering communications strategy across multiple disciplines, including media, digital, campaigns, stakeholder engagement and events. Strong track record of leading and developing senior managers and building high-performing, confident teams. Experience managing resources and budgets, including commissioning and working with agencies to enhance capacity and capability. Deep understanding of insight-driven, audience-led communications, including digital first approaches and modern communications practice. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍

item.title: Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Lead the UK's foremost voice for brand manufacturers, shaping policy, influencing key stakeholders, and driving the future success of a highly respected membership organisation. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ The British Brands Group (BBG) is the leading voice for brand manufacturers operating in the UK, representing many of the world's most recognisable consumer brands. ‍ We are seeking an outstanding Chief Executive Officer to lead the association, drive member engagement, shape policy and advocacy activity, and ensure BBG continues to deliver exceptional value for its members. ‍ This is a unique opportunity to lead a highly respected membership organisation, working with senior business leaders, policymakers, regulators, and industry stakeholders to influence issues of critical importance to the UK's consumer goods sector. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities: ‍ Lead the overall management and strategic development of BBG, ensuring the organisation operates effectively and delivers against its objectives. Act as Company Secretary, supporting the Chair and Council and ensuring strong governance and compliance. Build and maintain strong relationships with members, government, regulators, trade associations, and other key stakeholders. Lead the development and delivery of BBG's policy, public affairs, and advocacy agenda. Oversee the delivery of member engagement activities, including working groups, events, seminars, and training programmes. Manage the organisation's finances, external suppliers, and day-to-day operations, ensuring long-term sustainability and growth. ‍ ‍ Key Requirements: ‍ Senior leadership experience gained within a trade association, membership body, public affairs consultancy, or similarly stakeholder-led environment. Highly self-motivated with the proven ability to operate independently in a lean organisation. A strong understanding of UK public policy, regulation, and advocacy. Experience supporting boards, councils, or governance structures. Outstanding stakeholder engagement, influencing, and communication skills. Strong commercial, operational, and financial management capability. The credibility and judgement to engage effectively with senior executives, government officials, regulators, and policymakers. ‍ ‍ This is an exceptional opportunity for an experienced and politically astute leader to shape the future of an influential trade body at the heart of the UK's consumer goods sector. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information, please consult the detailed candidate pack and apply with a supporting statement and CV via the form below. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Integrated Communications Manager
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a globally respected membership body at the forefront of maritime innovation to recruit anIntegrated Communications Manager. ‍ For more than 160 years, the Royal Institute of Naval Architects (RINA) has advanced the art and science of naval architecture through technical excellence, thought leadership and global collaboration. Today, it brings together professionals across more than 140 countries working across everything from green propulsion and AI to warship resilience and autonomy. ‍ This is a newly created role at the heart of a fast-moving marketing and communications team during a significant period of organisational transformation and growth. ‍ The Integrated Communications Manager will lead integrated email marketing, CRM-led communications and cross-organisational campaign planning -helping grow membership, increase readership of The Naval Architect , expand specialist newsletter audiences and support wider business development activity. ‍ This is not a traditional hard-sell marketing role. RINA’s approach is “helpful first, selling second”: using high-quality content, audience insight and carefully targeted communications to build trust, engagement and long-term member value. ‍ The successful candidate will combine strong copywriting and campaign instincts with hands-on experience across CRM and email marketing. Maritime experience is not required, but curiosity, commercial instinct and an ability to engage credibly with technical subject matter are essential. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities include: ‍ ‍ Leading integrated email marketing andcommunications campaigns across membership, publications and businessdevelopment Managing CRM segmentation, automation andaudience targeting Creating and overseeing an organisation-wideemail communications calendar Supporting cross-sell and upsell campaigns andconversion journeys Working closely with content, events andpublications colleagues to maximise audience engagement Tracking campaign performance and usinginsight to improve effectiveness and conversion ‍ ‍ We are particularly interested in speaking with candidates fromintegrated communications, CRM, membership marketing, publishing, subscriptionor B2B content-led environments. ‍ RINA offers a highly flexible and collaborative working culture,with significant scope for creativity, experimentation and visible impact. ‍ Closing date: Friday 12 June Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so earlyapplications are encouraged. We will only be able to considerapplications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs,communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm,we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. Wepromise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are anequal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession toensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you havenot done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we canmake the right adjustments and considerations should they be required.

item.title: Industry Relations Manager
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to be supporting a nationally recognised organisation at the forefront of one of the UK’s largest infrastructure and consumer engagement programmes. The Opportunity: ‍ This is a highly collaborative and externally focused role within the Corporate Affairs function, offering the opportunity to work closely with senior stakeholders across industry, regulation and partner organisations. ‍ The Industry Relations Manager will lead engagement activity with key industry partners, providing insight, coordination and strategic support across a range of programmes and initiatives. You will monitor sector developments, identify implications for stakeholders and support the delivery of collaborative projects that contribute to long-term organisational objectives. ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys building relationships, navigating complex stakeholder environments, and translating technical or policy information into clear, actionable insight. Main responsibilities: ‍ Build and maintain strong relationships with industry stakeholders, acting as a trusted point of contact. Support the development and delivery of stakeholder engagement strategies and collaborative projects. Monitor sector, regulatory and policy developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities. Produce clear briefings, presentations and insight to support internal decision-making. Work cross-functionally with communications, insights and corporate affairs teams to align stakeholder activity. Represent the organisation at external meetings, forums and industry events. Support and develop junior colleagues, helping ensure high-quality delivery across the function. ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience delivering stakeholder engagement or industry relations activity within a complex environment. Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including presenting complex information clearly. Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Ability to interpret policy, regulatory or operational developments and communicate implications effectively. Experience working within a regulated sector or complex stakeholder environment would be advantageous. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Tuesday2 nd June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ EllwoodAtfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications,policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of External Affairs
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with The Health Foundation on the appointment of a Head of External Affairs. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a high-profile opportunity to lead external affairs, media relations and stakeholder engagement for one of the UK’s most respected and influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting into the Deputy Director of Communications, the Head of External Affairs will lead a busy and high-performing team responsible for media, public affairs and stakeholder relations activity across the Foundation’s broad portfolio of work. ‍ The successful candidate will play a central role in shaping and delivering the Foundation’s influencing strategy - identifying opportunities to position the organisation at the forefront of political, policy and public debate across health and care. ‍ Working closely with senior leadership, the role will provide strategic counsel across media, government relations, reputational issues and stakeholder engagement, while helping ensure the Foundation’s work reaches and influences key audiences nationally. ‍ This role would suit an experienced communications, public affairs or external affairs leader who combines strong political and policy awareness with excellent judgement, leadership capability and highly developed stakeholder management skills. ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead the Foundation’s external affairs, media relations and influencing activity across health and public policy issues. Develop high-profile and timely opportunities to position the Foundation at the centre of policy, political and media debate. Oversee engagement with senior stakeholders across Westminster, Whitehall, media and the wider health sector. Lead and develop the Foundation’s media relations strategy, ensuring strong profile and impactful coverage across target audiences. Provide strategic communications and public affairs advice to senior leaders and colleagues across the organisation. Develop compelling messaging, briefings and external affairs materials for a range of audiences. Lead, motivate and develop ahigh-performing external affairs team. Contribute to the wider leadership of the Communications function as a senior member of the management team. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading external affairs, media, communications or public affairs functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of delivering profile, impact and organisational influence through integrated external affairs strategies. Excellent stakeholder management skills with the ability to build credibility at senior levels. Highly accomplished written and verbal communication skills. Experience leading and developing teams within fast-paced and high-profile environments. Ability to manage complex issues with sound judgement, discretion and political awareness. Interest in health policy, public policy and mission-led work. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.title: Deputy Director of Communications
item.description: ‍ ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Health Foundation on the appointment of a Deputy Director of Communications. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a significant senior leadership opportunity within one of the UK’s most respectedand influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Communications, the Deputy Director of Communications will play a central role in shaping and delivering integrated communications strategies across the Foundation’s major thematic programmes of work. ‍ The successful candidate will work closely with senior leadership across the organisation to ensure communications activity delivers impact, strengthens organisational influence and supports the Foundation’s ambition to be one of the leading voices in UK public policy. ‍ The remit is broad and highly strategic - spanning communications strategy, stakeholder engagement, external affairs, reputation, thought leadership and executive counsel. The role also sits on the Foundation’s Senior Leadership Team and plays an important role in the wider leadership of the Communications directorate. ‍ This opportunity would suit an experienced strategic communications leader with strong policy instincts, excellent leadership capability and experience operating within complex, high-profile or mission-led organisations. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead integrated communications strategies across major thematic programmes of work. Develop clear, compelling and influential messaging across a broad range of policy and organisational priorities. Work closely with senior colleagues to identify influencing, convening and stakeholder engagement opportunities. Provide strategic communications advice to the Chief Executive, directors and senior colleagues across the Foundation. Oversee communications planning and delivery across a range of high-profile projects and programmes. Represent the Foundation externally with media, political and stakeholder audiences. Lead and develop multidisciplinary communications teams. Contribute to the wider leadership and strategic direction of the Communications function and the Foundation more broadly. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading strategic communications functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of developing integrated communications strategies that deliver organisational impact and influence. Excellent stakeholder engagement and relationship-building skills. Strong written and verbal communication capability, including experience handling complex and sensitive issues. Experience advising senior leaders and operating at executive level. Leadership experience within fast-paced, high-profile or mission-led environments. Interest in health policy, health care and improving public outcomes. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.title: Senior Campaigns Officer
item.description: ‍ The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Campaigns Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Campaigns Manager and colleagues across policy, communications and public affairs, the Senior Campaigns Officer will support the delivery of integrated campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. ‍ The role will suit someone with strong political awareness, excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex policy issues into compelling campaign activity. You will thrive in a fast-paced environment, spotting opportunities to influence debate and helping deliver campaigns that achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present. ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Support the planning and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling campaign messaging and calls to action. Work collaboratively with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Support member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Monitor political, policy and media developments, identifying opportunities to advance the College’s position. Build productive relationships with external stakeholders and partner organisations. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to support continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Experience delivering campaigns, advocacy or influencing activity within policy, public affairs, healthcare, charities, membership bodies or related environments. Strong understanding of the UK political and health policy landscape. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop persuasive messaging for different audiences. Ability to translate complex policy or evidence into clear and engaging campaign activity. Strong organisational and project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities. Collaborative and confident working style with strong stakeholder engagement skills. Experience of digital campaigning tools or techniques would be beneficial. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Closing date: Friday 19th June ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Campaigns Manager
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of a Campaigns Manager. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help shape and deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Head of Press and Campaigns, the Campaigns Manager will lead a small number of focused, strategic campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. The role will suit someone who combines strong political and strategic instincts with creativity, pace and excellent stakeholder management skills. ‍ You will play a key role in embedding a more modern, delivery-focused campaigning function, working collaboratively across policy, communications and public affairs to deliver campaigns that cut through and achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present [LINK HERE] ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Lead the development and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling narratives and clear calls to action. Work closely with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Identify and respond quickly to political, media and stakeholder opportunities. Build productive relationships with external partners, stakeholders and healthcare organisations. Oversee member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Line manage and support the Senior Campaigns Officer. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to refine activity and demonstrate impact. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience delivering campaigns within policy, public affairs, healthcare, membership bodies, charities or related environments. Strong understanding of the UK political and health policy landscape. . Experience influencing decision-makers and managing stakeholder relationships. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop persuasive campaign messaging. Strong project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities at pace. Collaborative and confident working style with the ability to engage senior stakeholders and clinicians. Experience managing or supporting junior colleagues is desirable. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Closing date: Friday 19th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Communications Executive
item.description: ‍ About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield are proud to be supporting a leading transport and logistics company to recruit Communications Executive. The opportunity: ‍ Working as part of a collaborative communications team, you will support the planning, creation and delivery of content across a variety of channels. You will help ensure communications are clear, consistent and aligned with organisational priorities while contributing to initiatives that strengthen reputation and employee engagement. ‍ The successful candidate will be a strong communicator with excellent writing skills and the ability to tailor your approach for different audiences. You'll be organised, proactive and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ‍ Regular travel across the UK will be required to engage with colleagues, gather content and support communications activity across multiple locations. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Create and deliver engaging content across internal and external communications channels, including digital, social media and corporate platforms. Support the planning, coordination and delivery of communications campaigns and activities aligned to organisational priorities. Build relationships with colleagues across the business, travelling regularly across the UK to gather content, develop stories and support stakeholder engagement. Monitor media coverage and communications performance, providing insight and reporting to support informed decision-making. Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure consistent messaging and the timely delivery of communications materials, briefings and updates. ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience in communications, public relations, journalism, marketing or a related field. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Experience creating content for digital and social media channels. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Professional judgement and discretion when handling sensitive information. Willingness and ability to travel regularly across the UK. ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Application deadline: 9am on 17 th June ‍ ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Government Relations Manager
item.description: ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ Our client, a leading global technology company, seeks a Senior Government Relations Manager to join its small and highly effective UK public affairs team. You will work alongside senior leaders in the business to analyse and understand Government policies and maintain the organisation’s great reputation with politicians and other senior stakeholders. This role will support business objectives by helping to position the company as a trusted advisor to Government and policy-makers across a broad range of issues. ‍ ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Develop and execute the Government Relations programme, engaging with key political and industry influencers. Analyse Government policies and their potential impacts, providing constructive alternatives. Build and maintain relationships with Government, trade associations, and other senior stakeholders. Provide clear and concise briefings on policy developments and their implications. Coordinate internal communication on political monitoring and reporting. ‍ Key Skills and Experience ‍ In-depth understanding of the UK political process and policy development. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present complex ideas clearly. Experience in managing Government Affairs programmes in a large commercial organisation or public affairs agency. Strong relationship-building skills and ability to navigate multicultural environments. Experience of working in technology, telecoms or digital policy is highly desirable. ‍ Application ‍ This is a full-time position and we are particularly interested in hearing if you are immediately available or have a short notice period. Please apply by submitting your CV via the link below. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director of Campaigns
item.description: We are seeking an exceptional communicator to join our client’s Executive Leadership Team and lead the development of a bold, influential, and campaigns-led approach to advocacy, policy, and communications. ‍ This is a newly created leadership role with the opportunity to redefine how our client engages with Government, policy makers, the media, members, and key stakeholders. As Director of Campaigns, you will be responsible for ensuring the organisation is recognised as the authoritative and unified voice of the sector, driving meaningful policy outcomes and raising awareness of the industry's critical contribution to the UK economy. ‍ You will lead the integration of policy, public affairs, communications, and stakeholder engagement to deliver high-impact campaigns that influence decision-makers, strengthen reputation, and create lasting value for members. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, you will shape organisational strategy and drive a "campaigns first" culture across all advocacy activity. ‍ Working closely with colleagues you will create and deliver compelling campaigns that mobilise support, influence policy, and position the organisation at the centre of key national debates. ‍ This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced campaigns, public affairs, or policy leader looking to make a significant impact within one of the UK's most important sectors. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities include: ‍ Develop and deliver a strategic, insight-led campaigns programme that aligns with organisational priorities, drives member value, and delivers measurable policy and advocacy outcomes. Lead the development of evidence-based policy positions and thought leadership, translating complex issues into compelling narratives that influence political, regulatory, media, and industry audiences. Build and maintain influential relationships with Government, parliamentarians, regulators, policymakers, members, and industry stakeholders to strengthen the organisation's voice and impact. Enhance the organisation's external profile through strategic communications, media engagement, impactful storytelling, and acting as a senior spokesperson when required. Foster collaboration across membership, policy, communications, and external partners to maximise campaign engagement, amplify key messages, and strengthen sector-wide advocacy. Lead, develop, and inspire a high-performing team, ensuring effective resource management, accountability, innovation, and delivery against strategic objectives. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for a strategic and politically astute leader with a proven ability to deliver influential campaigns and shape public debate. ‍ Requirements include: ‍ Proven experience leading high-profile advocacy, public affairs, or stakeholder engagement campaigns, ideally within a trade association, membership organisation, professional body, or similarly complex stakeholder environment. Deep understanding of Government, Parliament, public policy, and political decision-making processes, with the ability to influence agendas and secure strategic outcomes. Exceptional communication, stakeholder engagement, and narrative-building skills, with the ability to translate complex issues into compelling and persuasive messaging. Strong media and external affairs experience, including acting as a credible spokesperson and building influential relationships across Government, media, regulatory, and industry networks. Demonstrable success in leading, developing, and inspiring high-performing teams within a fast-paced, results-driven environment. Commercially astute and strategically minded, with the credibility, judgement, and leadership capability to operate effectively at Executive Leadership Team level and build consensus across diverse stakeholder groups. ‍ ‍ This is a rare opportunity to build and lead a newly created function with significant strategic influence. You will help shape the future of an industry that underpins the UK's economy, leading campaigns that drive real change and ensure the sector's voice is heard where it matters most. ‍ If you are an ambitious and influential leader with a passion for advocacy, policy, and strategic communications, we would love to hear from you. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information and to apply, please share a copy of your CV via the form below. ‍ Deadline for applications is midday on Thursday 2nd July. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading membership body within the health and social sciences sector. ‍ The opportunity: ‍ As Digital Marketing Specialist, you will play a key role in delivering and optimising digital marketing activity across paid media and email marketing. This is a technical marketing role which combines PPC, paid social, email campaigns and marketing automation, with responsibility for driving brand awareness, audience engagement and conversion across a range of products and services. You will manage Google Ads activity, including Google Ad Grants, oversee paid social campaigns across multiple platforms, and work closely with agency partners to ensure strong performance and return on investment. You will use data and insight to inform decisions, track campaign performance, and report on results to identify opportunities for continuous improvement. You will contribute to email marketing campaigns, customer journeys and content creation, working collaboratively with colleagues across the organisation. This is an excellent opportunity for a digitally savvy marketer to make a visible impact within a purpose-driven organisation while helping to shape and develop its digital marketing capabilities. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Plan, deliver and optimise PPC and paid social campaigns across Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Conduct audience targeting, keyword research, A/B testing and campaign optimisation to improve engagement and conversion. Set up tracking and reporting using GA4 and other analytics tools, providing actionable insights and recommendations. Create and deliver email marketing campaigns, automated customer journeys and compelling digital content, while collaborating with stakeholders on wider marketing initiatives. Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to support integrated marketing campaigns and drive audience engagement. Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven experience managing PPC and paid social campaigns across platforms including Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Strong knowledge of digital marketing analytics, conversion tracking, campaign reporting and performance optimisation. Experience delivering email marketing campaigns, automation journeys and audience segmentation strategies. Solid understanding of SEO, CRO, digital best practice, GDPR compliance and emerging marketing technologies. Highly analytical and commercially minded, with excellent communication, copywriting and stakeholder management skills. How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ The deadline for applications is Monday 26th June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Manager (Prudential Reporting and Tax)
item.description: The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading trade association which represents the interests of members across a broad spectrum of financial services. Working closely with industry leaders, government, regulators and the media, the organisation champions the developments of a thriving sector which serves society and ensuring that the UK retains its role as a global leader in financial services. ‍ The opportunity ‍ The trade association are looking for a Policy Manager to support the Prudential Reporting and Tax team in its engagement with members and external stakeholders across government, banks and regulators to lead on various area of policy development relating to prudential reporting and tax rules. ‍ ‍ The role will be involved in undertaking evidence gathering and content development contributing to reports, thought leadership and cross-cutting initiatives such as media content, blogs and online material. This will include items such as the Total Tax Contribution document, Plan for Growth documents and other reports. The role will also offer opportunities to deputise for principles where needed and run individual workstreams as required as well as briefing and drafting of related papers, media content, blogs and online material. Key Responsibilities ‍ Own relationships with subject matter experts and members on subjects relating to prudential reporting and tax. Supporting development of written responses to consultations discussion papers issued by the Prudential Regulation Authority, the Bank of England, HM Treasury, HMRC, the Department of Business and Trade and other external stakeholders. Ensuring policy proposals are supported by credible and integrated research. Working closely with media and communication teams to raise awareness of policy positions and priorities. Key skills ‍ Ability to respond to member needs whilst actively seeking opportunities to innovate and add value. A strong working knowledge of the UK banking landscape including knowledge of the challenges and opportunities impacting the future of the UK banking industry. A track record of providing strategic analysis at pace and synthesise information and complex concepts into meaningful actions or proposals. Works collaboratively across all internal departments to harness skills and experience from across the business to deliver high quality services. Excellent interpersonal skills, the ability to work, and build relationships, with various stakeholders. Strong verbal and written communication skills, clearly and accurately presents information using appropriate language to construct persuasive arguments and points of advocacy that is easy to understand. Comfortable using AI tools to enhance productivity and decision making in day-to-day work. ‍ How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Tuesday 30th June 9:00am
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Manager (Commercial Finance)
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading trade association which represents the interests of members across a broad spectrum of financial services. Working closely with industry leaders, government, regulators and the media, the organisation champions the developments of a thriving sector which serves society and ensuring that the UK retains its role as a global leader in financial services. ‍ The opportunity ‍ The organisation is looking for a Policy Manager to join the Commercial Finance team at a significant moment for the sector with an unprecedented focus on business growth and access to finance. The role represents an exciting opportunity to influence the future regulatory frameworks around business lending and shape the narrative around high profile issues such as Covid loan schemes. ‍ As a Policy Manager, you will play a pivotal role in delivering against the organisation’s policy priorities in collaboration with its members - ranging from the largest UK retail banks to smaller and specialist lenders, as well as non-bank alternative lenders. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Acting as subject matter expert on specific areas of policy such as the regulation of SME finance and government loan schemes. Providing expertise in areas including prudential regulation, smart data, restructuring and insolvency, economic crime and digital assets. Supporting the running of key member groups, including active participation at such meetings and deputising for senior colleagues. Supporting relationship management with members, associate members and key stakeholders (government departments, British Business Bank, Financial Conduct Authority, business groups and others). Preparing consultation responses and other advocacy, briefings, newsletter updates, blogs and presentations. Analysing data and economic insights to inform advocacy and provide actionable insights. Horizon scanning of developments such as matters that could impact Commercial Finance, identifying the relevance for members and proactively considering action. ‍ ‍ Key Skills ‍ Proven experience in financial services or a related sector, a trade body/member organisation, professional services or government/civil service/regulator. Experience of a relevant regulatory regime such as consumer credit or a proven ability to quickly gain proficiency in complex regulation. Strong attention to detail with an ability to communicate complex information in an accessible manner to a broad audience. Ability to identify key political developments and analyse their impact on members, whilst working in tandem with members to create appropriate policy responses. Strong research skills, ensuring policy proposals are supported by credible evidence. ‍ ‍ How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Friday 3 rd July 9:00am
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Communications and Engagement Officer
item.description: ‍ About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a respected regulatory organisation operating in the public interest. The opportunity: ‍ The Communications and Engagement Officer will work closely with colleagues across the organisation to deliver integrated communications activity that aligns with strategic objectives, strengthens reputation, and supports employee engagement. ‍ This is a varied and hands-on communications role within a small, collaborative team, contribute to both internal and external communications while supporting stakeholder engagement activity. You will support the delivery of impactful communications campaigns, create engaging content, support media relations, and build strong relationships with a wide range of stakeholders. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Develop and deliver integrated internal and external communications activities and contribute to communications planning. Build and maintain positive relationships with key stakeholders and support engagement initiatives. Create engaging content across a range of channels, including websites, social media, newsletters, briefings, and campaign materials. Prepare briefing materials and stakeholder communications for senior leaders. Monitor, analyse and evaluate communications activity, using data and insights to inform future campaigns and engagement strategies. Experience within life sciences, healthcare, innovation or policy-led sectors would be advantageous. ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with an understanding of media relations and communications planning. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to tailor messages for different audiences. Experience delivering both internal and external communications campaigns across multiple channels. Strong copywriting and briefing skills, with a talent for storytelling and translating complex information into clear, compelling content. Confident using digital communications platforms and content production tools to support communications objectives. Highly organised and proactive, able to manage multiple priorities and work collaboratively. ‍ ‍ How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ The deadline for applications is Friday 3 rd July 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. item.title: Senior Director, Corporate Communications (Europe)
item.description: The organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering exclusively with a global payments technology leader to find a Senior Director, Corporate Communications for Europe. Our client is operating at the heart of digital commerce and financial services, with a significant presence across European markets. This is an organisation that sits at the centre of major conversations shaping the future of payments, regulation, and economic growth. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ As Senior Director, Corporate Communications (Europe), you will lead integrated communications programmes that support business priorities and strengthen the organisation’s reputation across Europe. Working in close partnership with Government Affairs, Risk and Legal teams, you will drive corporate media, external relations, and reputation strategy across multiple markets. This role requires strong judgement in high-profile situations, the ability to operate across multiple European markets at pace, and a proven track record of advising senior leaders. ‍ The role will lead a small but high-performing team, manage agency relationships and budgets, and act as the primary point of contact for issues management and crisis communications across Europe. ‍ ‍ Experience required: ‍ Extensive experience in corporate communications, public affairs, and issues management Experience in payments, financial services, technology, or other regulated industries Strong track record advising senior executives in high-stakes situations Proven ability to lead communications across multiple European markets Excellent writing and communication skills with the ability to craft compelling narratives on complex issues Ability to operate at pace, manage multiple priorities, and work effectively in fast-moving environments Strong leadership, team management, and stakeholder management capability ‍ ‍ If you are a senior communications leader with a passion for high-impact work in a fast-moving, regulated industry, and the drive to shape reputation strategy at a European level, we look forward to hearing from you. ‍ ‍ Benefits: ‍ Office based in Central London ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Policy & Standards
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of a Head of Policy & Standards. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy, standards and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater impact across the issues affecting surgeons, dental professionals and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in setting standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and shaping debate across major healthcare, workforce and regulatory challenges. ‍ As the College continues to strengthen its position as the leading voice of surgery, this role will play a critical part in ensuring its policy and standards work remains authoritative, evidence-based and influential. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced policy leader to shape the future of surgery and dentistry across the UK and internationally. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Communications and with four direct reports, the Head of Policy & Standards will be responsible for setting the strategic direction of the College's policy and standards agenda. The successful candidate will oversee the development of policy positions, professional guidance and standards that influence decision-makers, support the profession and ultimately improve patient care. ‍ The successful candidate will ensure policy and standards work translates into real-world impact, identifying emerging issues, generating evidence, responding rapidly to developments and working closely with colleagues across campaigns, communications and engagement to influence change. ‍ The role offers the opportunity to work closely with senior clinicians, healthcare leaders, regulators, policymakers and government stakeholders while helping shape the future direction of one of the UK's most respected professional bodies. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Lead the strategic development of the College's policy and standards agenda across surgery and dentistry. Oversee horizon scanning to identify emerging political, clinical, workforce and regulatory developments. Translate complex evidence, data and expert insight into clear and influential policy positions and recommendations. Ensure policy development is evidence-based, academically rigorous and focused on delivering impact. Lead the development and governance of professional standards, guidance and frameworks. Build and maintain relationships with government departments, regulators, healthcare organisations, professional bodies and international partners. Represent the College in high-level policy discussions, consultations, inquiries and stakeholder forums. Work closely with campaigns, communications and engagement colleagues to maximise the impact of policy and standards activity. Provide strategic advice to senior leadership, Council and the Faculty of Dental Surgery Board. Lead, develop and support a high-performing Policy & Standards team. ‍ ‍ ‍ Person specification: ‍ Significant experience leading policy development within healthcare, government, regulation, membership organisations, professional bodies or related environments. Demonstrable experience translating complex evidence, data and stakeholder insight into clear policy recommendations. Strong understanding of healthcare policy, regulation, professional standards and workforce challenges. Experience engaging and influencing senior stakeholders, including policymakers, regulators, clinicians and healthcare leaders. Proven ability to develop strategic policy positions and deliver high-impact policy outputs. Experience managing teams and creating high-performing, collaborative working cultures. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including briefing senior leaders and external stakeholders. Strong analytical skills, sound judgement and the ability to operate effectively in a complex and fast-moving environment. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Wednesday 15 July 2026 We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help organisations hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
