item.title: Executive Strategic Support Lead
item.description: The Opportunity: ‍ ‍ We are partnering with a notable charity in the education sector to recruit an Executive Strategic Support Lead. We are seeking a proactive, highly organised and strategically minded professional to provide executive support to the CEO, while also contributing to organisation-wide strategic projects. This is a 12-month role at the heart of the organisation, combining executive support, external engagement coordination, and strategic delivery. The emphasis of the role will shift over time from external engagement preparation to greater focus on strategic development work. Key Responsibilities: ‍ Support organisational strategic development projects including business planning, impact frameworks, and cross-organisational improvement initiatives Provide executive support to the CEO and Deputy CEO team, including, briefing packs, prioritisation, and coordination across SLT Manage preparation and briefing for Deputy CEO external engagements including government, sector, and media activity Coordinate inputs across teams to produce high-quality briefings, reports, and decision documents Support internal communications and leadership coordination across the organisation Track and manage external engagement activity across systems and ensure follow-up actions are delivered Support effective decision-making by synthesising complex information into clear, actionable insights ‍ Key Skills Required: ‍ ‍ Experience supporting senior leaders (e.g. Chief of Staff, Executive Assistant, Private Office, or senior operations role) Strong ability to manage competing priorities in fast-paced, complex environments Excellent written communication and briefing skills for senior audiences Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence across senior teams Highly organised with experience managing board meetings, agendas, and action tracking Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a delivery focus Experience in government, public sector, charity, or mission-driven organisations (desirable) High discretion, judgement, and ability to handle sensitive information Ability to translate complexity into clear, practical outputs ‍ ‍ If this role sounds of interest, please apply via our website. Please note we are unable to process applications submitted by email due to high demand. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Communications Manager
item.description: Closing date: 16 July 2026 Location: London (hybrid working) Reporting to: Director, Media and Communications Department: Corporate Affairs and Strategy Salary: Competitive ‍ About the ABPI ‍ The Association of the British Pharmaceutical Industry (ABPI) exists to make the UK the best place in the world to research, develop and access medicines and vaccines to improve patient care. We represent companies of all sizes that invest in discovering and developing medicines and vaccines that enhance and save the lives of millions of people around the world. ‍ Our members and affiliates include established research-based pharmaceutical companies, specialist service providers supporting pharmaceutical R&D, and organisations that work closely with the pharmaceutical industry across the UK. Working in partnership with government, regulators and the NHS, we help patients gain faster access to innovative treatments and vaccines. We also collaborate across the wider life sciences ecosystem to make the UK an attractive place to invest, innovate and grow. ‍ We are committed to building an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and value the unique skills and perspectives each individual brings. ‍ We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from suitably qualified candidates from all backgrounds. If you require any adjustments throughout the recruitment process, please let us know and we will be pleased to support you. ‍ Job purpose ‍ Reporting to the Director of Media and Communications, the Senior Communications Manager plays a leading role within the ABPI's communications function, helping to shape and deliver the organisation's media relations, messaging and external engagement. The role sits at the heart of a high-profile, fast-moving press office operating within the UK life sciences and pharmaceutical policy environment. The successful candidate will bring significant experience across strategic communications, including: ‍ Proactive and reactive media relations Political and policy communications Stakeholder engagement Digital and social media Senior spokesperson support The postholder will operate with a high degree of autonomy and deputise for the Director of Media and Communications where required. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced communications professional looking to progress towards a Director-level role within one of the UK's leading trade associations. ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Strategic communications leadership ‍ Work closely with the Director of Media and Communications to develop and implement the ABPI's communications strategy. Help shape the organisation's external narrative across key policy and government affairs priorities. Deputise for the Director at senior internal meetings, member forums and external engagements. Support team planning and help manage workload across the communications function. ‍ Media relations and press office ‍ Lead day-to-day media relations, including proactive story generation, reactive enquiries and journalist engagement. Oversee the quality and accuracy of press releases, statements, briefing notes and media materials. Develop relationships with national, broadcast and trade journalists. Monitor breaking news, parliamentary developments and policy announcements, ensuring timely communications responses. Participate in the out-of-hours media rota. ‍ Proactive campaigns and policy communications ‍ Lead communications campaigns supporting key policy priorities including: Commercial environment Patient access Health data Clinical research UK life sciences competitiveness Translate complex technical and regulatory issues into compelling communications. Identify proactive media opportunities across ABPI activity. Develop media lines and supporting briefing materials. Lead communications activity around key ABPI events and conferences. Digital, social media and content ‍ Oversee social media activity across LinkedIn and other relevant platforms. Work with the Head of Digital to maintain engaging website content. Commission and produce written, digital and video content. Monitor performance, report on KPIs and use insight to improve communications activity. ‍ Spokesperson support and stakeholder engagement ‍ Prepare briefing packs, Q&As and talking points for senior spokespeople. Support media interviews and provide briefing and feedback. Build relationships with: Member communications teams Patient organisations NHS communications teams Government press offices Other external stakeholders Provide communications advice across the organisation. ‍ Skills, experience and qualifications ‍ The successful candidate will demonstrate: Significant experience in a senior media and communications role, either in-house or within an agency. Strong strategic and operational media relations experience. Proven experience handling reactive and crisis communications. Excellent relationships with national, broadcast and specialist media. Outstanding written and verbal communication skills. Experience preparing and supporting senior spokespeople. A sound understanding of the UK political and policy landscape. Experience delivering digital and social media communications. Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities. Experience within health, life sciences or pharmaceuticals is desirable but not essential. Candidates should demonstrate an ability to quickly understand complex policy issues. A collaborative working style, sound judgement and confidence operating with senior stakeholders. Inclusion ‍ The ABPI is committed to creating an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone who works with us, visits our offices or engages with our content. We provide: Step-free access Hearing support Accessible restrooms Accessible digital content We are PIF TICK certified, ensuring our health information is evidence-based, trusted and accessible. We also actively seek to work with partners and suppliers who share our commitment to accessibility and disability inclusion. ‍ How to apply ‍ For the full job description, salary and benefits information, or to apply for this opportunity, please send your CV together with a covering letter to: Joshua Gray Joshua@ellwoodatfield.com or submit via the online portal Closing date: 16 July 2026 ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Interim Policy and Public Affairs Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced and ambitious Interim Policy and Public Affairs Manager to lead a high-profile programme of policy development and influencing focused on protecting vulnerable customers' financial security. This role offers the chance to shape national policy and legislative reform at a critical time, leading work on family law reform, financial services policy and consumer protection. You will develop evidence-based policy positions, influence decision-makers across government, Parliament, regulators and industry, and work with people with lived experience to deliver meaningful systemic change. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Lead the development of evidence-based policy positions, consultation responses and policy recommendations on strategic priorities. Manage stakeholder engagement across the legal, financial services and social care sectors to build consensus and influence policy reform. Develop and deliver public affairs strategies to influence government, Parliament, regulators and industry stakeholders. Draft high-quality consultation responses, policy papers, parliamentary briefings and other influencing materials. Organise and facilitate stakeholder roundtables, parliamentary events and ministerial engagement. Build and maintain strong relationships with senior stakeholders across government departments, regulators, professional bodies, financial services organisations and partner organisations. Ensure the voices and experiences of survivors are embedded throughout policy development and influencing activity. Monitor policy, legislative and regulatory developments, identifying opportunities to advance organisational priorities. Work collaboratively with communications colleagues to support external influencing campaigns and policy launches. Provide policy advice to senior leadership and contribute to organisational planning and strategy. Represent the organisation externally at meetings, conferences and events. ‍ Key Requirements ‍ Significant experience in policy development and public affairs, ideally in a complex stakeholder environment. Proven experience delivering successful influencing campaigns leading to policy, legislative or regulatory change. Experience writing high-quality consultation responses, policy papers and evidence-based recommendations. Experience engaging and influencing senior stakeholders, including government ministers, officials, parliamentarians, regulators and industry leaders. Strong understanding of UK government, parliamentary and policy-making processes. Knowledge of family law and/or financial services and consumer policy. Understanding of domestic abuse, and/or work with vulnerable customers. Excellent analytical skills with the ability to translate complex issues into clear, practical policy solutions. Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, including drafting persuasive policy and briefing materials. Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, remote working environment. ‍ If this role is of interest please apply via the link on this advert and not via email, as some applications may be missed. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Public Affairs, Policy and Membership Manager
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with Together for Short Lives, the UK’s leading charity for children living with serious illness, their families, and the services that provide them with palliative care. ‍ Together for Short Lives supports families directly through practical guidance, emotional support and financial assistance, while also bringing together children's hospices, NHS services and health and care professionals to improve standards of care across the UK. ‍ Alongside families, members and partners, Together for Short Lives plays a leading role in influencing government policy and campaigning for better access to children's palliative care. It works closely with ministers, parliamentarians, NHS bodies and healthcare leaders to secure sustainable funding, shape national policy and improve services for children and families. The charity also leads research, develops best practice guidance and represents the voices of families and professionals to help drive long-term improvements across the sector. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ The Public Affairs, Policy and Membership Manager will play a key role in shaping their influencing strategy, supporting evidence-led policy work, and lead membership engagement to ensure their community feels valued and informed. ‍ Reporting to the Associate Director of External Affairs & Membership, you will monitor political and policy developments, develop briefings and consultation responses, and build relationships with members, parliamentarians, government officials and sector partners to help improve access to high-quality children's palliative care across the UK. ‍ This is a varied role combining strategic policy work with stakeholder engagement and membership leadership. The successful applicant will thrive in a fast-paced environment, bring strong public affairs or policy experience, excellent relationship-building skills and the confidence to engage with senior stakeholders. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Lead Together for Short Lives' public affairs, policy and influencing activity, helping shape national policy to improve children's palliative care. Monitor political and policy developments, producing briefings, consultation responses and campaign materials for members and stakeholders. Build and maintain relationships with MPs, government officials, NHS bodies and sector partners, while supporting the APPG for Children Who Need Palliative Care. Represent the charity externally, translating research and evidence into impactful policy recommendations and influencing opportunities. Support the strategic development of the membership programme and line manage the Membership and Engagement Officer. Manage the organisation's finances, external suppliers, and day-to-day operations, ensuring long-term sustainability and growth. ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven public affairs or policy experience, with the ability to influence government and shape policy. Strong understanding of the UK political landscape, with a passion for current affairs and an interest in healthcare, charities or membership organisations. Strong stakeholder engagement skills, building relationships with MPs, government officials, NHS bodies and sector partners. Excellent written and analytical skills, with experience producing policy briefings, consultation responses and evidence-based recommendations. Previous line management or experience or the confidence to step into a management role. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ The deadline for applications is Thursday 16 July 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Interim Communications Specialist
item.description: The Opportunity: ‍ ‍ We are partnering with a public sector organisation to recruit an Interim Communications Specialist on a 6–9-month assignment. This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role combining strategic advisory, diagnostic analysis, and practical implementation planning, with potential to support early-stage execution. ‍ Key Responsibilities: ‍ Conducting a comprehensive review of current communications activity Identifying gaps, and designing a refreshed, fit-for-purpose communications strategy aligned to organisational priorities ‍ Key Skills Required: ‍ Proven experience as an Interim Communications Lead / Specialist / Consultant Strong background in both internal and external communications Demonstrable experience delivering: Communications diagnostics and gap analysis Communications strategy design and implementation Change communications in complex organisations ‍ Experience working in public sector and/or regulated environments Experience delivering or advising on rebranding projects Ability to operate at both strategic and operational level Comfortable working in complex, politically sensitive environments ‍ If this role sounds of interest, please apply via our website. Please note we are unable to process applications submitted by email due to high demand. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Public Affairs – Financial Services
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a leading organisation operating within financial services, with a significant public profile and an ambitious growth agenda. As part of a wider corporate affairs and communications function, the organisation is investing in its external engagement capability and is seeking an experienced public affairs leader to shape and elevate its public affairs strategy. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a pivotal leadership role for a commercially minded public affairs professional who enjoys combining strategic thinking with hands-on delivery. You will lead the development of an ambitious public affairs strategy, working closely with senior executives to ensure external engagement supports wider business priorities and long-term growth. ‍ Alongside leading a small team, you will play a key role in strengthening the organisation's external profile, building influential stakeholder relationships and identifying opportunities to shape policy conversations in a meaningful way. ‍ ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Develop and deliver a proactive public affairs strategy aligned to organisational and commercial objectives. Provide strategic counsel and brief senior executives on political and policy developments. Lead, coach and develop a small public affairs team, fostering a collaborative and high-performing culture. Build and maintain trusted relationships across Westminster, Whitehall, trade associations and other key external stakeholders. Manage external public affairs agencies, ensuring high-quality delivery and measurable outcomes. Monitor the evolving political landscape, identifying emerging risks and opportunities. Work collaboratively with colleagues across communications, marketing, brand and wider business functions to deliver integrated campaigns. Manage budgets and ensure public affairs activity demonstrates clear value and impact. ‍ ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Significant experience leading public affairs within an in-house team, consultancy, trade association or comparable environment. Proven experience gained within financial services is preferred. Strong understanding of UK politics and the policy landscape, with experience engaging senior political and policy stakeholders. Confidence advising and influencing senior executives, with experience operating in high-profile environments. A strategic thinker who is equally comfortable rolling up their sleeves to deliver day-to-day activity. Proven leadership experience, with a track record of coaching and developing others. Commercial awareness and an understanding of how public affairs can support organisational growth and reputation. Collaborative, proactive and intellectually curious, with excellent relationship-building and communication skills. ‍ ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. Please note: Candidates will be required to sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) to engage with this process. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Policy Manager
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ London & Partners is the growth agency for London, working to support the city’s economy, attract investment, drive innovation and help businesses scale. Working across high-growth sectors and in partnership with business, government and industry, London & Partners plays a central role in promoting London as a leading global city for investment, talent and innovation. Its work focuses on building a more sustainable, resilient and inclusive economy for the capital. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ London & Partners is seeking a Senior Policy Manager to play a leading role in shaping policy and thought leadership focused on London’s economic growth. Sitting within the Policy and Impact Unit, the Senior Policy Manager will develop insight-led policy ideas and market interventions that support growth sectors, innovation and business development across the capital. The role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects spanning areas such as innovation, climate, life sciences, AI and business growth, while engaging closely with senior stakeholders across government, industry and the wider London ecosystem. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Develop policy insights, thought leadership and market-focused recommendations to support London’s economic growth and high-growth sectors Lead and manage complex policy and strategy projects, ensuring delivery to agreed time, budget and quality standards Analyse economic, sector and policy trends to identify opportunities, barriers and practical solutions Build and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders across government, business, think tanks and academia Produce high-quality briefings, reports and presentations, and represent London & Partners at meetings, events and stakeholder forums. ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience ‍ Strong experience in economic growth, innovation or sector-focused policy roles within government, a think tank, business organisation or related environment Proven ability to develop influential policy ideas, thought leadership and evidence-based recommendations Excellent project management skills, with experience leading complex programmes and coordinating multiple stakeholders Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly for different audiences Experience building relationships with senior stakeholders across policy, government and industry. ‍ ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please share your CV using the link below before the closing deadline on Thursday 16th July 2026. ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Adviser
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with the Confederation of Passenger Transport (CPT) in the search for a Policy Adviser. The CPT is a renowned membership body which exists to help the bus and coach industry to provide better journeys for all. We do this by representing around 800 members ensuring the issues that matter most to the industry are firmly on the political agenda, giving them practical advice and support, and bringing the industry together to build better solutions. Our dedicated teams in England, Scotland and Wales work closely with members at local and national levels to help them achieve the best possible operating environment. ‍ Our members include the largest multi-national transport operations along with small family run businesses. We also represent 150 suppliers who each provide dedicated products and services to the bus and coach industries. ‍ ‍ Working at CPT ‍ Our values matter to us. They help us achieve what our members have asked us to and deliver our very best both individually and as an organisation. We are: ‍ Ambitious to make things better for the passengers our industry serves Inclusive in seeking out different perspectives and; Always there when our members need us ‍ We ask every colleague to live to these values as part of their role and ask managers to enable their teams to do so. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This role comes at an exciting time to be working in the bus and coach sector. Our industry is essential to supporting economic growth, thriving communities and opportunities for all, and this is increasingly recognised among policy makers and opinion formers. ‍ CPT works closely with government at all levels to help the industry deliver to its full potential, and as a Policy Adviser you will build strong ties with the Department for Transport and other key stakeholders to develop and promote impactful policy initiatives which advance the priorities of CPT members and to ensure the sector’s interests are represented and reflected in government policy. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Develop policy papers and briefings, including collation of relevant third-party research, to influence national and regional decision makers and those that influence them. Engage with government departments to help deliver CPT’s policy goals, ensuring the organisation’s policy priorities are clearly understood by decision-makers and CPT is recognised as the lead expert organisation on bus and coach issues. Work with CPT members to seek views on policy and to gather evidence to support strategies and priorities, including collaborating with members’ in-house teams to make sure policy reflects the needs of the bus and coach industry. Develop authoritative and evidence-based submissions such as consultation responses and select committee submissions with member input and engagement. Provide policy support for CPT Regional Managers on key strategic policy issues to ensure CPT’S policy positions are understood at the local and regional level. Monitor, collect and utilise data relevant to the sector to: ensure CPT’s evidence base is up to date; inform CPT’s external communications activity; identify trends and emerging issues; develop dashboards and visualisations for colleagues and members; and support policy development and strategic decision-making. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Excellent written communicator with strong attention to detail and experience writing reports, presentations and briefings. Ability to identify and communicate key challenges in a clear, informative, succinct yet impactful way. Skilled in using quantitative data to identify issues and trends, support a policy position, strengthen an evidence-base and tell a story. A self-starter with strong organisational skills with an ability to work to tight deadlines and work strategically. Experience in navigating public policy issues and problem solving either within government or as a key government stakeholder and influencer. Adept at maintaining relationships with multiple stakeholders with the ability to manage multiple viewpoints and to influence. Ability to think strategically and see the ‘bigger picture’. Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills. Experience of overseeing the delivery of research projects by contracted third parties. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Friday 31st July 9:00am We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
